question:
Will South Africa take second place in Miss Universe 2025?

Description: The Miss Universe 2025 competition is scheduled to be held in Thailand on November 21, 2025. You can find more information about that here: https://www.missuniverse.com/the-competition-thailand/

This market will resolve according to the listed country represented by the contestant named first runner-up in this competition. 

If the result of this competition isn't known by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on official results as reported by the Miss Universe Organization.

evidences.title: Here's When & Where to Watch the 2025 Miss Universe Pageant in ...
evidences.content: For audiences in the United States, the 2025 Miss Universe pageant will be available to stream via Telemundo and Peacock on Nov. 20. But you won’t have to get up early to watch it. Instead, the finals will take place at 8 a.m. in Thailand on Nov. 21. That means coverage will begin at 8 p.m. ET/ 7 p.m. CT/5 p.m. PT in the U.S. on Nov. 20. Preliminary coverage on Telemundo starts an hour before the show. The contest will be held at the exhibition and convention center Impact Challenger Hall in Pak Kret, Nonthaburi, Thailand. Carlos Adyan, Danilo Carrera, Jessica Carrillo, and Jacqueline Bracamonte will be the hosts. [...] And if you’re interested in the preliminary and National Costume Competitions, those will be airing Wednesday, November 19. They will be livestreamed on the Telemundo App, the @telemundoentretenimiento YouTube and Telemunfo Al Día FAST Channels. It will be at 1 p.m. Thailand time. That’s 1 a.m. ET. Related ### Miss Mexico Fátima Bosch Wins Miss Universe 2025 Miss Universe 2025 has been at the center of controversy this year, after Miss Mexico Fátima Bosch was called “dumb” by pageant executive Nawat Itsaragrisil during a pre-competition event that was streamed li...

evidences.title: Melissa Nayimuli | Miss Universe South Africa 2025
evidences.content: Hello, my name is Melissa Na. I am 27 years old and I'm from in the eastern plane. If I were to be any animal, I would want to be one of these dogs because I mean, come on. The superpower I would love to have is teleportation. My role model is my mother. The nickname I had that I hated when growing up was O and Lee. Didn't understand that. And my claim to fame is definitely the big hair. I'm not sure how big I get, but we'll keep on going. And my favorite dance move is definitely the my story is not unique to just xenophobia. Anyone who has felt marginalized or treated unfairly due to gender, race, sexual orientation can relate to me where if you've ever felt in your life as if you've had to fight for your space, hide who you are in order to be accepted in any space. That is the story you [...] It doesn't discriminate. It knows no borders. You will help whoever is in need. You need to understand what the job is before you ask someone why they want to be that. So, I will carry on this legacy and I will state my claim in this position cuz this is who I am. My name is Melissa Nancy, a proud South African and a proud Ugandan woman. [Music] Nothing is ever just black and white. Diver...

evidences.title: South Africa’s Zozibini Tunzi crowned Miss Universe
evidences.content: Zozibini Tunzi was crowned Miss Universe in Atlanta, GA on Sunday (Dec. 8). “I grew up in a world where a woman who looks like me, with my kind of skin and my kind of hair, was never considered to be beautiful,” the South African beauty said during the pageant. “I think that it is time that that stops today.” Tunzi’s win marks the first time a Black woman has won Miss Universe since Leila Lopes in 2011 and the first time in history that Miss Teen USA, Miss America, Miss USA and now Miss Universe have all been Black women. During the pageant, Tunzi advocated for teaching young girls the importance of leadership. [...] “It’s something that has been lacking in young girls and women for a very long time—not because we don’t want to, but because of what society has labelled women to be,” she said. “I think we are the most powerful beings on the world, and that we should be given every opportunity… And that is what we should be teaching these young girls—to take up space.” Oprah Winfrey congratulated the 26-year-old winner on Twitter. “Congratulations Miss South Africa, the new Miss Universe [Zozibini Tunzi]!” she wrote. “Agree with you…leadership is the most powerful thing we should...

evidences.title: Miss Universe Winners: Every titleholder from the present ...
